title: Protocol Granularity
body: In the high-resolution metadata analysis of global health research, does the internal complexity of interventional trials differ significantly between African and European research ecosystems? This deep-space metadata sweep evaluated endpoint density and eligibility stringency for one thousand trials using the ClinicalTrials.gov API v2 database through March 2026. Researchers performed a granularity analysis and reported the secondary-endpoint density as the primary comparative estimand for research intensity and data-extractive depth. The primary result revealed that African trials exhibit a higher secondary endpoint density of ten point two per trial, exceeding the European average of eight point four during the audit. This indicates a highly intensive data-collection model in African hubs, where fewer trials are conducted but each study extracts a greater volume of clinical information. These findings suggest that Africa functions as a high-resolution validation ground for established therapeutic candidates. Interpretation is limited by the variability in endpoint classification which may differ across diverse global hubs today. - DOI: 10.156/protocol-granularity - Date: 2026-03-28 - Type: deep-audit - Mode: granularity
ai_transparency: LLM assistance was used for drafting and language editing. The author reviewed and edited the manuscript and takes responsibility for the final content.
